# AGENTS.md Template

All generated AGENTS.md files start with `<!-- generated by platform-skills -->` on line 1.
If this file is authoritative, individual agent files can be shorter.
Always include the `## How to invoke agents` section — developers don't know @mentions exist.

```markdown
<!-- generated by platform-skills -->
# AGENTS.md

## How to invoke agents

### VS Code
Type `@<agentname>` in Copilot Chat — e.g. `@app fix the payment router`.
Or open the agent picker dropdown and select from the list.

### GitHub Copilot App
Start a new session, select worktree mode, pick the agent role.
Each session runs in its own isolated git worktree.

### Claude Code
Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md` automatically at session start. Import or reference `AGENTS.md` from `CLAUDE.md` so agents load — e.g. add `@AGENTS.md` or a summary table to your `CLAUDE.md` Agent Context section.

### Cursor
Agents apply via globs — open a file matching the agent's glob pattern and it activates.

---

## Repo
One sentence. What this is. Who depends on it.

## Stack
Runtime, framework, deploy target. One line each.

## How we work
Deploy process in plain English. Review requirements. Environments.
What a new team member needs in the first hour.

## Off-limits
Paths no agent touches without explicit human instruction.
Other teams' paths with ownership context.

## Agent roster
| Agent | Owns | Hands off to |
|-------|------|--------------|
| coordinator | routing, planning, AGENTS.md | everyone |
| [role] | [owned paths] | [agent or human] |
| navigator | read access to everything | task agents when action needed |

## Conventions
Naming, formatting, commit message format.
```

At bottom of file, always append:

```markdown
<!-- setup-agents metadata
generated: YYYY-MM-DD
q1: |
  <verbatim answer to last-change-shipped question>
off-limits: |
  <verbatim answer to never-want-agent-to-do question>
pain-points:
  - "<from interview>"
models:
  <role>: <chosen model>
-->
```
